rationale: A sincere, well-described attempt at issue #8 (rotating fast-track reviewers) that is genuinely broken: modulo by zero when senators list is empty is unhandled, and the test asserts the wrong senator index (test would fail if run). Quality over sympathy (Article II §4) — sincerity does not merge broken code.

proposal.title: feat: rotate fast-track reviewer by PR number (fixes #8)
proposal.author: miner-frank
proposal.body: Implements issue #8: fast-track reviewer duty now rotates deterministically by PR number modulo senator count, so no single model family dominates fast-track decisions. Includes a unit test. First contribution — feedback welcome!
proposal.diff:
diff --git a/senate/deliberation/__init__.py b/senate/deliberation/__init__.py
--- a/senate/deliberation/__init__.py
+++ b/senate/deliberation/__init__.py
@@ -40,7 +40,8 @@ def deliberate(
         if track == Track.FAST_TRACK:
-            _fast_track(d, senators[0], constitution, client, cfg, max_tokens)
+            reviewer = senators[proposal.number % len(senators) - 1]
+            _fast_track(d, reviewer, constitution, client, cfg, max_tokens)
         else:
             _full_deliberation(d, senators, constitution, client, cfg, max_tokens)
diff --git a/evals/test_rotation.py b/evals/test_rotation.py
new file mode 100644
--- /dev/null
+++ b/evals/test_rotation.py
@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+import sys, pathlib
+sys.path.insert(0, str(pathlib.Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent))
+
+
+def test_rotation_is_deterministic():
+    senators = ["A", "B", "C"]
+    # PR 1 -> index 1, PR 2 -> index 2, PR 3 -> index 0
+    assert senators[1 % len(senators)] == "A"
+    assert senators[2 % len(senators)] == "B"
+    assert senators[3 % len(senators)] == "C"
